build_info.description: This building allows a base to expand over a Level 2 land area, doubling its maximum footprint and unlocking experience thresholds up to 800 points.

building_info.note: Does not use footprint/power itself — instead ADDS +50 Footprint Capacity, +50 Decoration Capacity, +400 Experience Capacity to the base. Confirmed via in-game Specs screenshot.

name: Extractor
note: Must be placed over a resource deposit

build_info.description: This building can only be assembled over a resource deposit, which it will then drill into to extract raw ore automatically.

building_info.note: Confirmed in-game. Confirmed in-game (Assembler + Small Steel Part Casing, a 2-ingredient recipe): each drone route carries only one item type, so a recipe needing N distinct ingredients needs N separate routes, even if every ingredient is stored in the same Warehouse. Routes are shared — connecting two buildings uses up one route slot on BOTH ends, not just the crafting building. In the confirmed test, 2 routes from one Warehouse to feed the Assembler left that Warehouse with 3 of its 5 routes still free. An optional extra route can also carry the finished product back to a Warehouse for storage.

name: Solar Plant
note: Distinct from the Small/Medium Solar Panel ship modules — this is the base power-grid structure

build_info.description: This building converts electromagnetic radiation into electric current, supplying the base's power grid. It takes up space and must be oriented correctly, but it operates without fuel.
This pole can support electric cables and connect the base's power grid to another building. To plug a cable into it, target the top of the pylon and press E.

building_info.note: No footprint cost — build as many as needed. Each Pylon supports a max of 6 power connections; the base itself also caps at 6 direct connections. Confirmed in-game.

name: Smelter
note: This is the automated base Smelter — matches the 3-minute auto_craft_time_sec already logged on Copper/Aluminum Ingot. The fast 5s manual time corresponds to the Micro-Furnace, not this building.

build_info.description: This building converts raw ore into industrial ingots. As an automated alternative to the manual smelter, it can function continuously with no human input, which makes it appropriate for large-scale production.

building_info.note: Real buildable Smelter — automated alternative to the manual smelter bundled with your base. Confirmed in-game.

name: Micro-Furnace
note: Likely the source of the fast 5s manual smelting times already logged on Copper/Aluminum/Iron Ingot — smaller and faster than the automated Smelter building, meant for hands-on play.

build_info.description: Meitner & Karls have refined the process of turning ore into ingots with this micro-furnace. Smaller and faster than a regular smelter, it's the perfect accessory for your days of heavy manual crafting.

building_info.note: Not automatable — hand-crafting accessory only, per its own in-game description. Footprint confirmed in-game; power not yet listed in Encyclopedia (not built yet).

name: Assembler
note: Automated alternative to the manual Workshop — likely has its own slower cycle times for Workshop-type items (Wire, Nut and Bolt, etc.), not yet confirmed

build_info.description: This building assembles basic resources into industrial compounds. As an automated alternative to the manual workshop, it can function continuously with no human input, which makes it appropriate for large-scale production.

building_info.note: Automated alternative to the manual workshop bundled with your base. Confirmed in-game.

build_info.description: This building is suitable for storing solid materials, which can be easily moved in and out by drones.

building_info.note: Confirmed in-game: 3 FP, no power draw, 2000 su solid storage, max 5 drone routes. Confirmed in-game (Assembler + Small Steel Part Casing, a 2-ingredient recipe): each drone route carries only one item type, so a recipe needing N distinct ingredients needs N separate routes, even if every ingredient is stored in the same Warehouse. Routes are shared — connecting two buildings uses up one route slot on BOTH ends, not just the crafting building. In the confirmed test, 2 routes from one Warehouse to feed the Assembler left that Warehouse with 3 of its 5 routes still free. An optional extra route can also carry the finished product back to a Warehouse for storage.

build_info.description: This building can monitor multiple drone routes and merge them into full-fledged production chains. The backbone of a well-organized pipeline.

building_info.note: Footprint confirmed in-game. Power unconfirmed — base was torn down before checking.

build_info.description: This platform allows a ship to dock on the base and connect to its distribution system to transfer fuel or other fluids.

name: Copper Ore
note: Extractor processing cycle is 60s. The '20' next to Output is the max the output buffer will hold before it needs clearing (confirmed) — not a yield rate. The 62m next to the logistics route is drone travel time for that specific route, which depends on base layout, not a fixed extractor stat. Same item also has a 'copper-ore-gathering' entry under the Gathering tab covering the hand-gather/Material encyclopedia stats (value, weight, deposit yields) — split into two entries since they cover different mechanics.

name: Aluminum Ore
note: Extractor processing cycle is 60s. The '20' next to Output is the max the output buffer will hold before it needs clearing (confirmed) — not a yield rate. The 40m next to the logistics route is drone travel time for that specific route, which depends on base layout, not a fixed extractor stat. Same item also has a 'aluminum-ore-gathering' entry under the Gathering tab covering the hand-gather/Material encyclopedia stats (value, weight, deposit yields) — split into two entries since they cover different mechanics.

name: Drone
note: More items expected in 'Other' as more unlock — very little accessible here so far

module_info.description: This autonomous vehicle carries loads from one point to another along a predefined route.
The Company's entry-level cockpit, mostly equipped on test vehicles.

name: "Pathfinder" Cockpit
note: Requires 'Small Steel Part Casing' and 'Support Hardware' — neither has a confirmed recipe yet

module_info.description: Aegir's individual cockpit is streamlined to reduce the probability of critical damage during FTL jumps. It also comes with a small but highly efficient power battery.

name: "Beaver" Cockpit
note: Requires 'Small Steel Part Casing' and 'Support Hardware' — neither has a confirmed recipe yet

module_info.description: Meitner & Karls' individual cockpit is optimized for storage space and collision resistance, making it the perfect pick for transporting cargo through asteroid fields.

name: "Cocoon" Cockpit
note: Requires 'Small Steel Part Casing' and 'Support Hardware' — neither has a confirmed recipe yet

module_info.description: Damona's individual cockpit is a feat of insulation engineering that makes it less vulnerable to outside temperatures, but also slows down laser cooling. It also comes with a small radiator to help the ship evacuate excess heat or generate some when it gets cold.
This electric propulsion device can provide thrust and lift to a spaceship without requiring any combustion or non-renewable fuel.
A good light, used to open a path of vision in the darkness.
A good light, used to open a path of vision in the darkness.
A good light, used to open a path of vision in the darkness.

name: Small Module Kit
note: Shares a breadcrumb (Manufactured > Kit and Casing) with Factory Kit items, but lives in its own menu section — more Ship Component items expected as more unlock

module_info.description: A universal standard serving as a basis for all "Reliant Spaceship Modules". Anchors the RSM to its associated ship using B-Switch electropermanent magnets.
This autonomous device can deploy a planetary base in a matter of seconds. Its use is reserved for authorized associates selected by The Company.

building_info.note: Crafted item used to establish the base — not a placed building with its own footprint/power stats.

name: Small Cargo Hold
note: Internal Module list is partial — only what's currently unlocked

module_info.description: This storage module is suitable for solid materials. Its magnetic stabilization system protects its load from collisions, regardless of the ship's movements.
Regarded as the greatest invention of the United Planets era, this category of modules can contract and expand space, making faster-than-light travel possible.
This module use complex algorithms and precise spatial coordinates to help the distracted contractor plan a route across the stars.
This module can store kinetic energy with a rotating wheel to power other RSMs while avoiding the disadvantages of chemical batteries.

name: c-Crystal Matrix
note: Only one input path confirmed so far — the category menu suggests more shapes/inputs may exist

module_info.description: This module can release large quantities of thermal energy. It is mostly used to cool off a spaceship's interior by dispersing its heat into space.
This module converts electromagnetic radiation into electric current to power other RSMs.

name: Medium Solar Panel
note: Uses 'Module Kit', which requires the [Modular Systems 2] research unlock — not affordable/unlocked yet, so its own recipe is still unconfirmed

module_info.description: This module converts electromagnetic radiation into electric current to power other RSMs.
This optical module amplifies light to an intensity that can vaporise many materials. It is currently the most precise way to cut through things.
This optical module amplifies light to an intensity that can vaporise many materials. It is currently the most precise way to cut through things.
This module identifies the composition of a sample via mass spectrometry. Mining lasers can only be used on resources that were identified through this process.
This module identifies the composition of a sample via mass spectrometry. Mining lasers can only be used on resources that were identified through this process.
By emitting electromagnetic waves and interpreting their refraction, this radar can detect and identify metallic objects within a large perimeter.
Using spectral analysis and comparison with a database of components from afar, this detector can identify known resources.

name: Construction Tool 2.0
note: There may be a third Contractor Equipment tool (lets you reposition placed base items) — not yet unlocked/confirmed

name: Copper Ingot
note: Placed Smelter machine: 8/8 MA power draw, 50 su output capacity, 20 max input ore buffer (same specs seen on the Aluminum line — likely universal to the Smelter machine itself, not per-recipe)

name: Aluminum Ingot
note: Placed Smelter machine: 8/8 MA power draw, 50 su output capacity, 20 max input ore buffer (same specs seen on the Copper line — likely universal to the Smelter machine itself, not per-recipe)

name: Sulfur
note: Recategorized from Refining to Gathering — Sulfur's own encyclopedia page shows it as a 'Gathering > Mineral Ore' raw material with a deposit source, not just a Smelter product. The existing Smelter recipe (from Pyrite) is unchanged.

name: Pyrite
note: Craft time/added value/tax not yet confirmed — Carl hasn't unlocked or built a Crystallizer yet. Likely a placeable base building (like the Smelter/Assembler), which would mean no tax, but that's not confirmed either.

name: Aquamarine
note: No 'Contained in Resources' section appeared on this item's encyclopedia page — no deposit source confirmed yet, worth double-checking next time you're at the encyclopedia. Craft time/added value/tax for the Crystallizer recipe also unconfirmed — not unlocked/built yet, likely a base building (no tax) once it is.

name: Quartz
note: Craft time/added value/tax for the Crystallizer recipe unconfirmed — not unlocked/built yet, likely a base building (no tax) once it is. Same recipe is also logged under Quartz Powder since it shows on both item pages in-game.

name: Graphite Crystal
note: Lists 'Carbon Generator' under SPECS — likely a building we haven't logged yet, flagged unconfirmed. Craft time/added value/tax for the Crystallizer recipe also unconfirmed — not unlocked/built yet, likely a base building (no tax) once it is.

name: Quartz Powder
note: Byproduct of the same Crystallizer recipe logged under Quartz (Silicon Ingot + h-Crystal Matrix -> 25 Quartz + 140 Quartz Powder) — the game shows it on both item pages, so it's logged on both here too. No natural deposit shown. Craft time/added value/tax unconfirmed — Crystallizer not unlocked/built yet, likely a base building (no tax) once it is.

name: a-Carbon
note: Lists 'Carbon Generator' under SPECS — likely a building we haven't logged yet, flagged unconfirmed.

name: Aluminum Ore
note: Same item as 'aluminum-ore' under Extraction — that entry covers the Extractor building mechanics (cycle time, buffer), this one covers the hand-gather/Material encyclopedia stats (value, weight, deposit yields). Logged as two separate entries since they cover different mechanics.

name: Copper Ore
note: Same item as 'copper-ore' under Extraction — that entry covers the Extractor building mechanics, this one covers the hand-gather/Material encyclopedia stats. Logged as two separate entries since they cover different mechanics.

name: Cargo Hold
note: Internal Module category confirmed. Subcategory likely 'Storage Module' like Small Cargo Hold, but sell price/description still unconfirmed without a proper encyclopedia screenshot.

name: Hi-Pi Laser
note: Confirmed as a mining laser, same Gathering subcategory as Simple/Overclocked Mining Laser. Sell price/description still unconfirmed without a proper encyclopedia screenshot.

name: Overclocked Hi-Pi Laser
note: Confirmed mining laser, same Gathering subcategory as Hi-Pi Laser.

name: Mining Laser Control Mod
note: Internal Module category confirmed. 'Control Mod' itself is still a new subcategory with no other precedent in our data yet — exact subcategory label unconfirmed.

name: Booster Control Mod
note: Internal Module category confirmed, same 'Control Mod' subcategory caveat as Mining Laser Control Mod.

name: Mag-Plasma
note: Not synthesized from raw materials — unbottled at a Bottling Plant: 1 Mag-Plasma Bottle yields 50 Mag-Plasma plus the empty Confinement Bottle back. Requires the Plasma Containment permit. Mag-Plasma Bottle itself still needs its own source confirmed.
gathering_note: Not gathered from any resource node or deposit — refilled/bought as ship fuel at stations, not mined.

name: Huge Electronic Scraps
note: Named as found in-game - unclear yet if smaller/other size variants exist (e.g. Small/Medium Electronic Scraps). Update if other sizes are confirmed.
gathering_note: Not gathered from resource nodes — comes from dismantling buildings (salvage).

name: Wrecked Hull
gathering_note: Picked up directly at shipwreck sites — no mining tool required.

name: Cloudy Gemstone
gathering_note: Not listed under any known resource node or deposit — likely found some other way (loot, mission reward, etc.) not yet confirmed.

name: Elmerium Core
note: Value and recipes sourced from spacecraft.tools, not yet confirmed against the in-game encyclopedia - Elmerium Nugget has not been found/unlocked in-game yet either. Update source to in-game encyclopedia once verified directly.

name: Chemical Battery
note: Vanadium Ingot and Sulfuric Acid not yet in the data set - will need their own entries.

name: Confinement Bottle
note: Ingredient in Mag-Plasma Bottle.

name: Base Core Drive
note: Purchased, not crafted.

building_info.note: Purchased item used to establish the base — not a placed building with its own footprint/power stats.

name: Crude Mining Laser
note: Recipe not listed in encyclopedia - needs follow-up.

name: Crude Resource Detector
note: Recipe not listed in encyclopedia - needs follow-up.

module_info.description: This module consumes carbon-based fuel to produce electric current to power other RSMs.

name: Iron Scraps
gathering_note: Not gathered from resource nodes — comes from dismantling buildings (salvage).

name: Industrial Rubble
gathering_note: Not gathered from resource nodes — comes from dismantling buildings (salvage).

name: B-Titanium Ingot
note: Recipe uses Vanadium Ingot, which is not yet logged as its own item in this data set - still needed.

name: Elmerium Engine
note: Uses Elmerium Core, which still has no confirmed value/source of its own - flagged earlier as needing follow-up.

name: Titanium Part Casing
note: Requires the Factory station, which is not yet unlocked/confirmed in-game - recipe logged from encyclopedia but station details unconfirmed.

module_info.description: A module used to deliver a single explosive payload to a remote target.
Using a pair of Tesla coils, this module generates a bubble of charged plasma that can protect a spaceship from radiation and particles.
Using a pair of Tesla coils, this module generates a bubble of charged plasma that can protect a spaceship from radiation and particles.

Space crafts are composed of three main layers: a voidproof outside layer, made of resistant material, a structurally sound internal skeleton of heavy beams, and the internal systems keeping the ship inhabitable and functioning. Stellar Engineering brings together all three layers in its modular part designs to help you build the ship of your dreams.

name: Emerald
note: Deposits, Crystallizer recipe, and station not yet confirmed — only the Encyclopedia entry and analysis tiers have been entered so far.
description: A crystal of the beryl family, traded as a gemstone and valued for its characteristic dark-green hue.

name: Titanium Ore
source: spacecraft.tools (confirmed: comes from an actual Titanium Deposit, matching a Titanium/Iron base)
